title: The ⊨ (satisfies) symbol as heavy geometric slabs. ⊨ means 'satisfies' in logic: the definition satisfies all constraints. In Precept, the .precept definition must satisfy every stated constraint — verified at compile time before any instance can run. The vertical bar is the definition; the two symmetric horizontal arms are the constraints it satisfies. Unlike ⊢ (proves), ⊨ asserts that the definition is a complete model in which every governing rule holds.
conceptualBridge: The satisfies mark means Precept's definition is a verified model because ⊨ is the formal symbol for 'this structure satisfies all stated constraints' — compile-time verification is the proof that the definition ⊨ every invariant.
decision: Rejected: near-duplicate of 51-proves-mark — same slab construction, same logical symbol family (⊢ vs ⊨), similar H-shape at 32px; the ⊢ in 51 is the stronger representative with its asymmetric arms that differentiate it from a plain H; 57's symmetric arms make it less distinctive. Uniqueness score of 3 prevents promotion.
